授業目的: 本講義の目的は、（１）国際法の基本的な知識を習得すること、（２）その知識を、グローバルなあるいは⾝近な現実の問題を理解し解決するために活⽤する能⼒を育むこと、（３）英語で学術的な議論を⾏う⼒を養うこと、である。
到達目標: 次の3点を到達目標とする。（1）国際法の性質、法源、法主体や国際的な紛争解決の⽅法といった国際法の基本的な構造を理解すること。（2）国際法が、国際平和や⼈権保障等といった国際社会における共通の⽬的の実現に、どのように貢献しているかを理解すること。（3）国際法が、国際社会における現実の問題にどのように関連し、その解決にどのように貢献できるかについて、⾃分で考察し説明することができるようになること。
授業の概要・背景:
The first part of the course aims at providing students with basic knowledge of international law. The second part of the course is devoted to student presentations. Students choose a topic related to international law, consult with the lecturer about their plan in advance and speak for about 15-20 minutes in front of the class and answer questions both in person and online.
At the end of the semester, students are required to submit a short paper (at least 1,500 words) on the same topic as that of their presentations.
＊The schedule may be modified according to the size and composition of the class.
授業方法: This course is primarily conducted in person, although some sessions are also held online. If there are any changes to the course format, an announcement will be made in advance on LUNA.In all classes, students are strongly encouraged to take part in discussions by, for example, answering the questions posed by the lecturer, asking relevant questions, and commenting on presentations.

第1回:
【Startup Week】
"Online class（On-demand format）"／《50 minutes》

Introduction to the Course & Nature of International Law
- How does this course proceed?
- How does international law operate in international society?
第2回:
【Core Weeks①】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Sources of International Law
- What are the main sources of international law?
- How do treaties enter into force and how are they interpreted?
- What are the elements of customary international law?
- What is jus cogens (peremptory norm)?
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

States
- What are the requirements of a ʻStateʼ?
- What is ʻrecognition of Statesʼ and what is the legal effect of such recognition?
- What are State jurisdiction and immunity?
第4回:
【Core Weeks③】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

State Responsibility
- What is the Draft Articles on the Responsibility of States for Internationally Wrongful Acts (ARSIWA)?
- When does State responsibility occur?
- How is injury reparated?
第5回:
【Core Weeks④】
"Online class（On-demand format）"／《90 minutes》

International Dispute Settlement
- What are the various methods of international dispute settlement?
- What are the composition and procedures of the International Court of Justice (ICJ)?
- When does the ICJ have jurisdiction to examine a case?
第6回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（On-demand format）"／《30 minutes》

Preparation for Student Presentations and short papers
Prepare for your presentation and term paper (around 3 hours or more)
第7回:
【Core Weeks⑤】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Law of the Sea & International Human Rights Law
- What are the major maritime zones and what are the rights and duties of coastal States in each zone?
- How are maritime boundaries delimited?
- What is the Universal Declaration of Human Rights and what is its legal value?
- What are the core international human rights treaties and how does the monitoring mechanism in those treaties function?
第8回:
【Core Weeks⑥】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

Use of Force
- What acts are prohibited under Article 2 (4) of the United Nations Charter?
- What are the conditions for the lawful exercise of the rights of self-defense?
- What is ʻhumanitarian interventionʼ and is it lawful under international law?
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑦】
"Face-to-face class"／《90 minutes》

International Humanitarian Law & International Criminal Law
- How did International humanitarian law develop?
- What are the principles and core treaties regarding International humanitarian law?
- What are international armed conflict and non-international armed conflict?
- What are the composition and procedures of the International Criminal Court (ICC)?
- When does the ICC have jurisdiction to examine a case?
第10回:
【Flex Hours】
"Online class（Task-oriented format）"／《70 minutes》
Instructor Support for Student Preparations
第11回: Prepare for your presentation and short paper (around 3 hours or more)
第13回: Prepare for your presentation and short paper (around 3 hours or more)
第15回: Prepare for your presentation and short paper (around 3 hours or more)
第17回: Prepare for your presentation and short paper (around 3 hours or more)
第19回: Prepare for your presentation and short paper (around 3 hours or more)
第21回: Prepare for your short paper (around 3 hours or more)

教科書/Required texts0: Since I will assign readings from various textbooks and articles each week (particularly those listed under ‘Reference Books’ below), no specific textbook is designated for this course.
参考書/Reference books3: International Law: A Very Short Introduction
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.
General Foundation Courses (Basic Courses): 1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.
